title: Selection sort
content:
Haanji, bilkul. Main sources ke flow ko maintain karte hue aur har line ki detail ko follow karte hue Selection Sort se shuru kar raha hoon.

Yahan Step 1 ke detail notes hain:

Introduction to Sorting (Step 2.1)

  • Yeh Strivers A2Z DSA course ka lecture hai, jo India ka sabse in-depth course hai jisme 456 modules/problems solve kiye gaye hain.
  • Placement preparation ke liye Sorting techniques ka aana bahut zaroori hai.
  • Step 2.1 mein hum teen main algorithms cover karenge: Selection Sort, Bubble Sort, aur Insertion Sort.
  • Sorting ka doosra part (Step 2.2) recursion par based hoga jo agle lecture mein aayega.

1. Selection Sort

Concept: "Select Minimums"

  • Selection sort ka naam hi batata hai ki isme humein select karna hai. Hum kya select karte hain? Hum minimums select karte hain.
  • Logic: Poore array ko dekho aur sabse chota element (minimum) dhundho, fir usey array ki pehli sahi jagah par place kar do.

Step-by-Step Example (Array: 13, 46, 24, 52, 20, 9)

  • Step 1: Poore array mein minimum dhundho. Yahan 9 minimum hai. Is 9 ko pehli position (index 0) par rakho. Wahan pehle se 13 tha, toh 13 ab 9 ki jagah chala jayega (swap).
    • Array ab: 9, 46, 24, 52, 20, 13
  • Step 2: Pehla element sorted hai. Ab baaki bache unsorted array (46 se 13) mein minimum dhundho. Minimum 13 hai. Isse unsorted part ki pehli position (46 ki jagah) par swap karo.
    • Array ab: 9, 13, 24, 52, 20, 46
  • Step 3: Ab unsorted part 24, 52, 20, 46 hai. Minimum 20 hai. Isse 24 ke saath swap karo.
    • Array ab: 9, 13, 20, 52, 24, 46
  • Step 4: Unsorted part 52, 24, 46 mein minimum 24 hai. Isse 52 se swap karo.
    • Array ab: 9, 13, 20, 24, 52, 46
  • Step 5: Unsorted part 52, 46 mein minimum 46 hai. Isse 52 se swap karo.
    • Array ab: 9, 13, 20, 24, 46, 52
  • Observation: Agar 6 elements hain, toh array ko sort karne mein 5 steps lage. Aakhri element hamesha apne aap sorted position par hota hai.

Implementation Strategy (Pseudo-code Logic)

  • Hum swapping index 0 se shuru karte hain aur n-2 tak jaate hain.
  • Outer Loop: i chalega 0 se n-2 tak.
  • Inner Loop: Har i ke liye, hum i se lekar n-1 tak minimum dhundhte hain.
  • Finding Minimum: Shuruat mein hum maante hain ki mini = i (pehla element hi minimum hai). Fir loop chalakar check karte hain: if array[j] < array[mini], toh mini ko update karke j kar dete hain.
  • Swapping: Jab inner loop khatam ho jaye aur minimum mil jaye, toh usey array[i] ke saath swap kar dete hain.
  • Swapping Logic: Ek temporary variable ka use karke do numbers ko swap kiya jata hai: temp = a, a = b, b = temp.

Time Complexity

  • Pehli baar loop n baar chalta hai, fir n-1, fir n-2... aakhir mein 2 tak.
  • Yeh ek summation series banti hai: $n + (n-1) + (n-2) + ... + 2$. Iska formula $\frac{n(n+1)}{2}$ ke karib hota hai.
  • Isliye iski time complexity $O(n^2)$ hai.
  • Yeh Best, Worst, aur Average teeno cases mein $O(n^2)$ hi rehti hai.

Selection Sort ke notes complete hain. Agle algorithms (Bubble Sort aur Insertion Sort) ke liye jab aap "proceed" bolenge, tab main aage badhunga.




#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

void selection_sort(int arr[], int n) {
    // Outer loop: 0 se n-2 tak chalta hai [1]
    for (int i = 0; i <= n - 2; i++) {
        int mini = i; // Pehle element ko hi minimum maan lete hain [2]
        
        // Inner loop: i se n-1 tak minimum element dhundhta hai [2]
        for (int j = i; j <= n - 1; j++) {
            if (arr[j] < arr[mini]) {
                mini = j; // Naya minimum milne par index update karte hain [3]
            }
        }
        
        // Swapping: Minimum element ko sahi jagah (i index) par swap karna [4]
        int temp = arr[mini];
        arr[mini] = arr[i];
        arr[i] = temp;
    }
}

int main() {
    int n;
    cin >> n; // Array ka size input lena [5]
    int arr[n];
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) cin >> arr[i]; // Array elements input lena [5]

    selection_sort(arr, n);

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        cout << arr[i] << " "; // Sorted array print karna [6]
    }
    return 0;

}
